title: The Personality Brokers: The Strange History of Myers-Briggs and the Birth of Personality Testing
subtitle: A riveting history of the Myers-Briggs Type Indicator — its origins, its pseudoscientific foundations, and its astonishing cultural impact.
author: Merve Emre
themes: The unlikely story of the mother-daughter team who created the MBTI
The pseudoscientific foundations of personality typing
The cultural appeal of self-knowledge through categories
The tension between scientific validity and commercial success
The ethics of personality testing in hiring and education
awards: New York Times Book Review Editors' Choice
The New Yorker Best Books of the Year
excerpt: The Myers-Briggs Type Indicator is the most widely used personality test in the world — taken by 2 million people annually. But its origins are stranger than fiction. Merve Emre tells the story of Katharine Briggs and her daughter Isabel Myers, two amateur psychologists with no formal training who believed they had found the key to human understanding in Carl Jung's obscure theory of psychological types. The result is a compelling history of how a scientifically dubious instrument became a cultural phenomenon.
